content.text:
Let me analyze this record's expanded rendering against the four criteria.

The gloss: material is the stuff of which a thing consists — (a) that kind of matter such that every part of the thing is of it, and (b) on which the maker works in making the thing.

Rendering encodes:
- ref1 (the material) is a kind of thing.
- ALL parts of some thing (ref2) are of that kind (ref1) — this is the "every part is of it" clause.
- WHEN someone makes ref2 (inlined make: because someone did something at some time, ref2 exists; before, it didn't), that someone works on/does something to ref1 (the material).

This captures both criterial elements: kind-membership of all parts, and the maker acting on the material during the making. The genus (kind of matter/stuff) and differentia (all parts of it + worked-on-in-making) are present.

Self-flag: honestly admits substancehood (continuous mass, persistence of same stuff through making) is not captured, rendered as kind-membership of all parts + linkage to making. This is accurate — the rendering does drop mass-continuity and same-stuff persistence but keeps the criterial core. The flag is TRUE.

Gloss is scholarly: genus-differentia, sense-fixed, non-circular. No collision warnings.

The criterial core (distinguishing "material/what it's made of" from siblings) is carried: all-parts-of-that-kind + worked-on-in-making distinguishes material from e.g. shape or a mere ingredient.

{"id": "urn:molaug-v0:material", "verdict": "ACCEPT", "gloss_scholarly": true, "explication_carries_gloss": true, "self_flag_correct": true, "sense_collision_free": true, "reason": "Rendering carries both criterial elements (all parts of the thing are of that kind, and the maker works on the material in making); self-flagged lossiness re substance-continuity/persistence is honest and non-criterial; gloss is genus-differentia and non-circular."}

Let me analyze this record's expanded rendering against the four criteria.

The gloss: material is the stuff of which a thing consists — (a) that kind of matter such that every part of the thing is of it, and (b) on which the maker works in making the thing.

Rendering encodes:
- ref1 (the material) is a kind of thing.
- ALL parts of some thing (ref2) are of that kind (ref1) — this is the "every part is of it" clause.
- WHEN someone makes ref2 (inlined make: because someone did something at some time, ref2 exists; before, it didn't), that someone works on/does something to ref1 (the material).

This captures both criterial elements: kind-membership of all parts, and the maker acting on the material during the making. The genus (kind of matter/stuff) and differentia (all parts of it + worked-on-in-making) are present.

Self-flag: honestly admits substancehood (continuous mass, persistence of same stuff through making) is not captured, rendered as kind-membership of all parts + linkage to making. This is accurate — the rendering does drop mass-continuity and same-stuff persistence but keeps the criterial core. The flag is TRUE.
